title: Nohsngithiang Falls
tagline: Nohsngithiang Falls — heritage city of Meghalaya

overview.short: Nohsngithiang Falls (also known as the Seven Sisters Waterfalls or Mawsmai Falls) is a seven-segmented waterfall located 1 kilometre (0.62 mi) south of Mawsmai village in East Khas
overview.description: Nohsngithiang Falls (also known as the Seven Sisters Waterfalls or Mawsmai Falls) is a seven-segmented waterfall located 1 kilometre (0.62 mi) south of Mawsmai village in East Khasi Hills district in the Indian state of Meghalaya. The water falls from a height of 315 metres (1,033 ft) and has an average width of 70 metres (230 ft). making it one of the tallest waterfalls in India. The falls plunges over the top of limestone cliffs of the Khasi Hills only during the rainy season.

topPlaces.name: Kynrem Falls
topPlaces.description: The Kynrem Falls is located 12 kilometres (7.5 mi) from Cherrapunji in East Khasi Hills district in the Indian state of Meghalaya. It is situated inside the Thangkharang Park.

topPlaces.name: Cherrapunji
topPlaces.description: Cherrapunji ( ), also known as Sohra, is a sub-divisional town (Proposed District) in the East Khasi Hills district in the Indian state of Meghalaya. It was the traditional capital of ka hima Sohra (Khasi tribal kingdom).

topPlaces.name: Nohkalikai Falls
topPlaces.description: Nohkalikai Falls, sometimes Ka Likai, is the tallest plunge waterfall in India. The 340 metres (1,115 ft) waterfall is located in the Indian state of Meghalaya, near Cherapunji (now known as Sohra), one of the wettest places on Earth.

topPlaces.name: Umshiang Double-Decker Root Bridge
topPlaces.description: Umshiang Double-Decker Root Bridge is a living root bridge in Meghalaya, India. It can be reached via trekking 45 kilometres (28 mi), or 6500 steps, from Cherrapunji.

topPlaces.name: Wah Kaba Falls
topPlaces.description: Wah Kaba Falls is a waterfall located near Cherrapunji in Meghalaya, in the north eastern India. The waterfall descends from a steep rockface and drops approximately 170–190 metres into a gorge.

topPlaces.name: Wei Sawdong Falls
topPlaces.description: Wei Sawdong Falls is a three-tiered waterfall located in Cherrapunji, Meghalaya.

items.note: The Kynrem Falls is located 12 kilometres (7.5 mi) from Cherrapunji in East Khasi Hills district in the Indian state of Meghalaya. It is situated inside the Thangkharang Park.

Cherrapunji ( ), also known as Sohra, is a sub-divisional town (Proposed District) in the East Khasi Hills district in the Indian state of Meghalaya. It was the traditional capital of ka hima Sohra (Khasi tribal kingdom).

Nohkalikai Falls, sometimes Ka Likai, is the tallest plunge waterfall in India. The 340 metres (1,115 ft) waterfall is located in the Indian state of Meghalaya, near Cherapunji (now known as Sohra), one of the wettest places on Earth.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nohsngithiang Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nohsngithiang Falls is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nohsngithiang Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shillong Airport (Umroi) (57 km) and the nearest railway station is Guwahati Railway Station (105 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nohsngithiang Falls?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nohsngithiang Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nohsngithiang Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Nohsngithiang Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nohsngithiang Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kynrem Falls, Cherrapunji, Nohkalikai Falls, Umshiang Double-Decker Root Bridge, Wah Kaba Falls.
